subject: Re: Warp tomo reconstruction not matching IMOD
body:
Hey Alister and Warp-list,

Took me a month to get back to this...
The good news is that I got to import into Warp a different K3 dataset that
we had processed in IMOD and it worked perfectly, so our pipeline is
validated.

The bad news is that this one problematic dataset from my previous post is
still unsolved. I looked into all your suggestions (thanks Alister!), but
did not find anything wrong.
I think something about the way this data was processed in IMOD is causing
this issue, but don't know what. If anyone has any ideas, please let me
know.

Thanks again!

Best wishes,

--
Ricardo Diogo Righetto


Em seg., 20 de mar. de 2023 às 12:49, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com>
escreveu:

> Hey Ricardo,
>
> The possible differences between Warp and IMOD tomogram reconstructions are
>
> 1. flipped across the central xy plane due to a different interpretation
> of tilt-angle
> 2. shifted due to a different 3D origin (in Warp the 3D origin is implied
> by the tomogram dimensions, in IMOD it is implied by the reconstruction
> thickness and the 2D image dimensions)
> 3. Edge effects - Warp and IMOD handle data from ‘outside the tilt-image’
> differently, this can lead to differences in tomograms outside the FOV of
> the tilt images
>
> I suspect at least one, maybe both of 1,2 being the source of the apparent
> ‘warping’ you refer to - video attached for easier comparison of images
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
>
>
>
> On 20 Mar 2023, at 11:28, Ricardo Righetto <ricardorighetto@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
> Hi,
>
> I'm trying to reconstruct subtomograms in Warp for refinement with M. The
> tilt series were originally processed, aligned and reconstructed in IMOD,
> so I imported them into Warp. The data is from a K3 detector.
>
> In the debugging process we noticed that the tomo reconstructions in Warp
> don't quite match the IMOD reconstructions.
> In particular, there seems to be a slight difference in magnification or
> some kind of warping effect (no pun intended), please see PNG snapshots
> attached. I made sure that the .xf file used in both Warp and IMOD are
> identical.
> For a sanity check, I've also reconstructed the tomogram using
> relion_tomo_reconstruct_tomogram and it matches the IMOD reconstruction.
> My suspicion is that I'm doing something wrong in Warp regarding image
> dimensions or pixel size, but don't know what exactly.
> The headers of both tomogram reconstructions do match regarding dimensions
> and pixel size, please find them below (first Warp, then IMOD).
>
> Any idea what is causing this?
>
> Thank you!
>
> ***
>
> RO image file on unit   1 :
> ../../../WarpM/relion_bin2_k3_2023_2_4pix/tilts/reconstruction/tomo_10_17.14Apx.mrc

>
